reasoning: The tournament has reached the final, which is set between Spain and Argentina after Spain beat France 2-0 and Argentina came from behind to defeat England 2-1. Spain enters as the favorite (around -164, roughly 58% implied) on the strength of a 37-game unbeaten streak, elite midfield depth in Pedri and Yamal, and a dominant 13-1 goal aggregate through the knockout rounds. Argentina remain a serious threat as defending champions with proven knockout pedigree, Messi's playmaking (three assists in the last two games), and Emiliano Martinez in goal, though their run has relied on late comebacks. England are named the dark horse only nominally as the strongest of the eliminated sides—having pushed Argentina to the wire—but they and France are already out, leaving this a genuine two-horse race that tilts toward Spain's consistency.
